name: Lomi Lomi Tuna
recipeIngredient: 1 1/4 Pounds fresh Ahi (yellowfin) tuna, medium diced
3/4 Cup cucumber, diced, seeded and peeled
1/2 Cup ripe Italian Roma style tomato, seeded and diced
1/2 Cup sweet red onion, finely chopped
1/2 Teaspoon hot pepper sesame oil (or to taste)
1/4 cup fresh cilantro leaves, coarsely chopped
recipeInstructions:
In a non-reactive bowl, combine the Ahi, cucumber, tomato, onion, lime juice, oils and salt and pepper to taste. Cover and refrigerate for 30 minutes. Remove from refrigerator 20 minutes before serving and stir in cilantro. Serve on baked wontons garnished with green onions, toasted sesame seeds and Tobiko, if desired.
Yield: 8-10 servings as an appetizer
This is based on an appetizer mixture served universally throughout Hawaii. Add or substitute any other fresh, seasonal crunchy vegetable you can find such as jicama, fresh water chestnuts, parsnips, etc.
Baked Wontons:
Beat 4 egg whites with a tablespoon of water until frothy. With a pastry brush, lightly paint wonton skins with the mixture and arrange in a single layer on non-stick or parchment lined baking sheets. Bake in a preheated 375 degree oven for 5-8 minutes or until wontons are lightly browned and crisp. You can sprinkle brushed but unbaked wontons with sesame seeds, Kosher salt, poppy seed or whatever to add interest. Store in an airtight container for up to 3 days.
